content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 29, 1991

Samuel Siegel
City Attorney
City of La Puente
15900 East Main Street
La Puente, CA  91744

Re:  Your Request for Confirmation of
     Telephone Advice
     Our File No. A-91-204

Dear Mr. Siegel:

     This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of the telephone advice provided to John McClendon of your office
on March 26, 1991 regarding the duties and responsibilities of
Councilmember Manual Garcia under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     Your letter accurately reflects the advice provided.
Mr. McClendon stated that Manual Garcia, a member of the City
Council of La Puente, is also a member of the La Puente
Redevelopment Agency.  Councilmember Garcia's spouse is a member
of the board of a school district located in part within the City
of La Puente.  Mr. McClendon asked whether Councilmember Garcia
was disqualified from participating in governmental decisions
relating to a tax increment pass through agreement between the
City of La Puente and the school district of which his spouse is a
board member.

     We advised Mr. McClendon that the provisions of the Act do
not disqualify Councilmember Garcia from participating in
decisions affecting the school district merely because his spouse
is a board member of the school district.  This is so because any
income received by Mr. Garcia's spouse from the school district is
exempted from the definition of income in Section 82030(b)(2)
which states that, for purposes of the Act, income does not

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660


[illegible]

Our File No. A-91-204
Page 2


include salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received
from a state, local or federal government. Therefore, income
received by Mr. Garcia's spouse from the school district, a local
entity, is not disqualifying.

     Please note, however, that we only provide advice regarding
the provisions of the Act.  Other provisions of law may be
applicable to these facts.  Accordingly, at the time of our
telephone conversation, we referred Mr. McClendon to the Office of
the Attorney General for advice regarding provisions of law other
than the Act.  We now reiterate this advice.

     We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        By:  Bianca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

SH/BMB/aa
